title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: April 04, 2026: Geopolitical Tensions & Job Growth Shape Market Tone

sections.title: Market Pulse: Navigating Uncertainty with Mixed Signals

items.headline: U.S. Futures Dip on Holiday Ahead of Key Economic Data; Geopolitical Tensions Remain a Headwind
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures were slightly down by approximately 0.2% on Friday as markets observed the Easter holiday, with investors evaluating recent stronger-than-expected jobs data and persistent developments in the Middle East. The ongoing conflict in Iran continues to fuel geopolitical uncertainty, contributing to concerns about inflation and the job market. This environment is prompting a 'risk-off' sentiment, with investors increasingly favoring 'safe-growth' assets.

items.statContext: Up +0.1% on Thursday, +3.4% for the week (as of April 2, 2026).

items.summary: Franklin Covey Co. has been the top-performing stock in April, gaining significantly.

items.summary: Kodak continues its upward momentum into April.

items.summary: Spire Global has shown strong performance, potentially driven by renewed interest in the space sector.

items.headline: Lipocine Inc. Plunges on Safety Results
items.summary: Lipocine Inc. experienced a sharp decline following the release of topline safety and efficacy results for its LPCN 1154 in patients with postpartum depression.

items.headline: Pasithea Therapeutics Warrants Drop
items.summary: Pasithea Therapeutics Corp. Warrants fell significantly after the company announced its PAS-004 received Fast Track Designation from the FDA for Neurofibromatosis Type 1.

items.headline: Inovio Pharmaceuticals Declines Amidst Public Offering News
items.summary: Inovio Pharmaceuticals saw a substantial drop following its announcement of a proposed public offering.

items.headline: Defensive Sectors Gain Traction Amidst Market Volatility
items.summary: A 'barbell strategy' is emerging among investors, with a rotation into defensive sectors like Utilities and Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) seeking 'safe-growth' plays. Utilities climbed 0.6% and REITs surged 1.5% as of April 3, 2026, even as broader indices faced intraday swings. This shift is partly driven by the substantial power demands of generative AI data centers.

items.headline: March Job Growth Exceeds Expectations, Inflation Remains a Concern
items.summary: The U.S. economy added 178,000 jobs in March, a rebound from the previous month and significantly surpassing economist forecasts. The unemployment rate also edged down to 4.3%. However, core PCE inflation was confirmed at 3.1% year-over-year in January, remaining above the Federal Reserve's target, while Q4 2025 GDP was revised down to 0.7% annualized.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady in April
items.summary: Prediction markets indicate a high probability (97.7%) that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates unchanged after its April 2026 meeting, scheduled for April 28-29. Despite this, the long end of the yield curve is rising, influenced by growing federal deficits and persistent inflation uncertainty. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Continues to Impact Global Markets
items.summary: Escalating tensions in the Middle East, particularly the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, have caused a significant global supply shock, impacting energy markets, supply chains, and inflation dynamics worldwide. Oil prices have surged past $110 per barrel, leading to concerns about 'slowflation' or 'stagflation'. President Trump has intensified rhetoric against Iran, threatening infrastructure targets. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Software Sector Faces Indiscriminate Selling Amid AI Fears
items.summary: The software sector experienced a significant downturn in early 2026, with over $2 trillion wiped out in weeks due to fears that AI agents would erode traditional software revenues. This has led to indiscriminate selling, even impacting fundamentally strong companies that have reported strong earnings.
